slides.title: The Object Factory 🏭
slides.content:
An Interface is a blueprint that just *describes* an object.

A **Class** is a blueprint that actually *creates* the object, complete with initial data and built-in functions.

slides.title: The Class Declaration
slides.content: You declare a class using the `class` keyword. We establish properties at the top, and assign them values inside a special `constructor` function.

quizData.question: What is the purpose of the `constructor` inside a Class?

options.text: It is a special function that runs once when the object is first created to set up its initial data.
To compile the class into an interface.

quizData.explanation: The constructor runs immediately upon creation, allowing you to pass in initial starting values.

slides.title: Spawning Objects
slides.content: To actually use that blueprint and build an object, you use the `new` keyword followed by the class name.
slides.codeSnippet:
// We pass "Alice" and 100 into the constructor
let p1 = new Player("Alice", 100);

slides.title: Classes as Types 🏷️
slides.content:
In TypeScript, a Class automatically acts as its own Type!

If you want a function to only accept Player objects, just write `function heal(p: Player) { ... }`.
